news.headline: Garment Printer Ink rebrands to GPI Supplies, expanding into graphic printing industry, launching new website www.gpisupplies.com.
news.summary: A garment printing industry supplier, formerly known as Garment Printer Ink, has rebranded as GPI Supplies and unveiled a new website at www.gpisupplies.com on February 28, 2025. This rebranding signifies a new chapter for the company as it continues to serve its customers.

news.headline: NUHS honours 77 exceptional individuals and 4 teams driving change in healthcare, championing system of the future.
news.summary: A total of 77 outstanding individuals and four innovative teams have been recognized by the National University Health System (NUHS) for their contributions to revolutionizing healthcare. The NUHS, based in Singapore, is leading the way in bringing about this change. The honorees have been acknowledged for their exceptional efforts in shaping the future of healthcare.

news.headline: Chinese Composite PMI Actual 51.1 (Forecast -, Previous 50.1)
Chinese NBS Non-Manufacturing PMI Actual 50.4 (Forecast 50.4, Previous 50.2)
China February Manufacturing PMI 50.2: Forecast 49.9
Chinese NBS Manufacturing PMI Actual 50.2 (Forecast 49.9, Previous 49.1)
Honda's Alabama plant begins production of 2026 Passport and Passport TrailSport, with 4,500+ associates involved. New TrailSport model boasts rugged upgrades.
news.summary: Over 4,500 employees mark the commencement of production for the latest Honda Passport SUV, acclaimed as the most off-road capable model in Honda's SUV lineup. The Passport TrailSport variant has been dubbed as the ultimate Honda adventure vehicle, boasting enhancements like steel skid plates, heavy-duty recovery hooks, and all-terrain tires.

news.headline: Prescott College alum Danny Lopez honored with Tucson city park renaming by Dr. Barbara Morris, celebrating his impact as Tohono O'odham educator.
news.summary: In Prescott, Arizona on February 28, 2025, Dr. Barbara Morris, President of Prescott College, recognized the renaming of a park in Tucson to pay tribute to Danny Lopez, a notable alumnus of Prescott College. Danny Lopez, a member of the Tohono O'odham community, was an educator, mentor, singer, and storyteller known for inspiring numerous individuals. The park renaming honors his contributions and impact on his students.

news.headline: Teamsters in Massachusetts secure tentative agreement with Stop & Shop, preserving 900+ jobs with substantial raises and benefits.
news.summary: After facing a credible strike threat, Teamsters Local 25 has successfully secured a tentative six-year contract in Freetown, Mass., ensuring the retention of over 900 union jobs at Stop & Shop. The agreement includes substantial wage and benefit enhancements for the workers. This development safeguards the livelihoods of a significant workforce in the community and reinforces the commitment to labor rights and fair working conditions.

news.headline: WesBanco, Inc. acquires Premier Financial Corp., becoming a diversified bank holding company. Nasdaq: WSBC and PFC.
news.summary: WesBanco, Inc., a diversified multi-state bank holding company, based in Wheeling, West Virginia, declared on February 28, 2025, the completion of its acquisition deal with Premier Financial Corp. The successful closure of this agreement, involving WesBanco (Nasdaq: WSBC) and Premier (Nasdaq: PFC), marks a significant milestone for both entities. This transaction is expected to have a noteworthy impact on the financial landscape, with WesBanco expanding its reach by integrating Premier's operations into its existing framework. The specifics of the deal, including the financial implications and future plans resulting from the acquisition, are likely to generate interest and scrutiny from various industry stakeholders.

news.headline: Chicago Faucets joins IWBI, promoting wellness in buildings. Founded in 1901, leading US commercial faucet maker.
news.summary: Chicago Faucets, a prominent manufacturer of commercial faucets in the United States since 1901, recently disclosed its partnership with the International Well Building Institute (IWBI). By joining IWBI, the company aims to demonstrate its dedication to advocating for improved well-being in built environments. This collaboration signifies Chicago Faucets' pledge towards advancing the well-being of individuals within commercial spaces.

news.headline: BTC surges to $105K amid Fed assurance, banks embrace crypto. States propose BTC reserve bills. Trump Media eyes crypto move. Phantom backs SUI.
news.summary: Bitcoin has surged to a whopping $105,000 as concerns about the Federal Reserve diminish. Chairman Powell announces banks are now permitted to engage with cryptocurrency. Investment giant Sachs is considering establishing a strategic reserve of Bitcoin. Illinois and Indiana have presented a bill to introduce a Bitcoin reserve. The Texas Lieutenant Governor is pushing for the swift passing of a Bitcoin reserve bill. Candidates supported by a crypto PAC emerge victorious in Florida primaries. Trump Media is preparing to shift its focus to cryptocurrency. However, it is unlikely that a Bitcoin reserve will be established anytime soon, according to Polymarket. A potential Executive Order from Trump could disrupt the traditional 4-year cycle of Bitcoin, as suggested by Bitwise. Ethereum users have chosen Danny Ryan to lead the foundation. The CME Group is planning to launch options on Bitcoin Friday futures. Robinhood teases a potential introduction of crypto futures. Phantom has expressed support for SushiSwap. Tesla increased the valuation of its Bitcoin holdings in the fourth quarter. Digital Currency Group (DCG) has initiated mining operations. Binance is under investigation in Spain for alleged misappropriation.

news.headline: BTC nears $106k, ETH shines. EU won't hold BTC reserves. Stablecoin market cap surges past $200B. MicroStrategy set to surpass capital raise goals. Coinbase eyes CFTC approval for SOL futures. Kraken restarts crypto staking. Grayscale applies to transform XRP trust to ETF.
news.summary: Bitcoin hits a milestone of $106,000, with Ethereum showing strong performance amongst major cryptocurrencies. Christine Lagarde mentions EU nations won't hold BTC reserves. Michael Saylor graces the cover of Forbes. South Dakota introduces two bills proposing BTC reserve. USDT now compatible with Bitcoin's Lightning Network. The stablecoin market cap surpasses $200 billion. Tether working on MiCA compliance. Supply of SOL stablecoin up by 73% post TRUMP. MicroStrategy set to exceed capital raise goals, as Coinbase seeks CFTC approval for SOL futures. Kraken resumes crypto staking in 39 US states, while Apollo lauds tokenized private credit fund launch. Circle incorporates USDC on Aptos platform. SBF's parents request presidential pardon. SEC greenlights combined BTC & ETH ETF, with Grayscale filing to convert XRP trust to ETF.

news.headline: Crypto plunge on trade war, record liquidations at $2.3bn. ETH down 35%, BTC dominance up. $3bn unlocks in Feb, stablecoin TVL at $218bn. Altcoins suffer 50% loss. Indian crypto review, Utah BTC reserve bill, XRP snubbed. ETFs may attract $50bn by 2025, MicroStrategy raises $563m for BTC. Thailand to launch token trading platform, Uniswap v4 released. AstroKimchi premium high in S.Korea. Tether’s $13bn profit reported, UBS tests blockchain for gold investments. Coinbase buys ads platform, RUNE at 5-year low.
news.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced a sharp decline as the US trade war commenced, resulting in the most significant liquidations ever totaling $2.3 billion. Ethereum (ETH) led the losses among major cryptocurrencies with a 35% fall, while Bitcoin (BTC) dominance reached a nearly 4-year high. In February, $3 billion of unlocks are anticipated, primarily driven by Solana (SOL). The total value locked (TVL) in stablecoins surged to a new record of $218 billion. Altcoins witnessed a surge in buybacks following the liquidation event, with HYPE leading the charge. Asian crypto stocks took a hit, notably led by Metaplanet.Renowned investor Elliot predicts that the crypto bubble will inevitably burst. However, Bitwise believes that Bitcoin ETFs have the potential to attract over $50 billion by 2025. MicroStrategy successfully raised $563 million to purchase more Bitcoin. India is reassessing its stance on cryptocurrencies, while South Korea's BTC Kimchi premium reached a 3-year high. Tether reported a staggering $13 billion in profit.Utah is considering becoming the first state to pass a Bitcoin reserve bill, while XRP was notably absent from Hong Kong's list of approved cryptocurrencies. Coinbase expanded its portfolio by acquiring the advertising platform Spindl. UBS is testing blockchain technology for digital gold investments, and Thailand is planning to launch a new token trading platform.Stablecoins are gaining traction in emerging markets, finding a strong 'product-market fit.' Uniswap rolled out its v4 upgrade, and the token RUNE reached a 5-year low amid concerns of bankruptcy.

news.headline: BTC hovers near $100K amid China trade tensions. $400m liquidated in volatile day; possible $10b in total. US plans sovereign wealth fund. Lummis suggests fund may invest in BTC. Pro-crypto exec to head fund. Sachs hosts AI & crypto event. MicroStrategy pauses BTC buys. ETH raises gas limits since 2021. Eric Trump advocates for ETH. Hyperliquid surpasses ETH returns. WLFI moves $212m ETH to Coinbase. SEC requires leadership nod for probes. TRON, Movement Labs deny token swap with WLFI. CFTC investigates Cryptocom, Kalshi. Banks could restrict crypto exposure. Kraken gains MiFID license in EU. Coinbase approved in UK. WazirX to vote on changes. Coinbase under fire for $300m scam loss.
news.summary: Bitcoin remains below the $100,000 mark as China imposes tariffs on US goods, contributing to the liquidation of another $400 million within a highly volatile 24-hour period. Cumulatively, liquidations over the past day are estimated to have surged to potentially as high as $10 billion, according to Bybit. In the political sphere, President Trump is preparing to establish a US sovereign wealth fund, with Senator Lummis hinting at potential BTC investments by the fund. Additionally, Michael Lutnick, a prominent figure in the crypto community, is slated to lead the sovereign wealth fund. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs is scheduled to host a press conference focused on artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency today. The news also involves MicroStrategy halting its Bitcoin purchases for the first time in weeks, while Ethereum raises gas limits for the first time since 2021. Eric Trump suggests that it is an opportune moment to consider adding ETH to portfolios. Furthermore, Hyperliquid has outperformed ETH in terms of 7-day revenues. WLFI has transferred $212 million in ETH to Coinbase Prime, while TRON and Movement Labs have refuted claims of a token swap involving WLFI. On the regulatory front, the CFTC is looking into Cryptocom and Kalshi's contracts, while there are concerns that banks might restrict crypto exposures, as noted by TD Cowen. In terms of market expansions, Kraken has secured a MiFID license in the EU, with Coinbase receiving approval to offer crypto services in the UK. WazirX is preparing to vote on restructuring its operations. However, Coinbase has drawn criticism for reportedly losing $300 million to scams.

news.headline: Sovereign wealth funds are exploring bitcoin opportunities in the latest episode of UNDER EXPOSED (EP11). Tune in on Tuesdays at 12:00 PM EST on YouTube and X. #crypto #NFTs
news.summary: Tune in to UNDER EXPOSED for the latest coverage of significant macroeconomic news affecting cryptocurrency and wider markets. Hosted by TylerD, Deeze, and Peter Jennings, this show delves into the latest trends, trading activities, and narratives shaping the industry. The hosts engage in in-depth discussions on top sectors in crypto and NFTs, market movers, and their current investment decisions. Catch the live stream every Tuesday from 12:00 PM EST to 1:00 PM EST on YouTube and X.

news.headline: BTC down despite prediction of crypto's prosperous period. Senate introduces stablecoin bill. FTX to start payouts Feb 18. ETH/BTC at 5-year low. XRP ledger resumes after shutdown. Ohio senator proposes state BTC reserve bill. South Dakota eyes BTC reserve. $1.25bn more stables issued by Tether & Circle. Semler Scientific acquires $88.5m BTC. Sol Strategies acquires $41m SOL. Ondo Finance unveils treasury RWA platform. Bullish Global prepares for IPO. SEC to lighten crypto oversight. Enforcement-focused regulation ending at CFTC.
news.summary: BTC is down despite positive comments from Sacks on crypto’s future. He praises BTC as a strong store of value. A stablecoin bill is presented in the Senate. FTX will start payouts on Feb 18. The SEC is set to ease crypto regulation. Investors warned by SEC to purchase tokens at their own peril. CFTC will no longer regulate through enforcement, according to Pham. ETH/BTC ratio hits a 5-year low, with supply surpassing Merge levels. XRP ledger resumes after a 1-hour network stoppage. Ohio senator initiates the state’s second BTC reserve bill. South Dakota may become the first to establish a BTC reserve. Tether and Circle print an additional $1.25bn in stablecoins. Semler Scientific acquires $88.5m BTC. Sol Strategies purchases $41m worth of SOL. Ondo Finance unveils a treasury RWA platform. Bullish Global eyes an IPO.

news.headline: BTC dominance increases as gold reaches new all-time high. Key updates: MicroStrategy rebrands, Blackrock to launch BTC ETP in Europe, CBOE eyes ETH Options, BTC could hit $500k by 2028.
news.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies remain stable in the market, with Bitcoin's dominance on the rise. Notable updates include business intelligence firm MicroStrategy rebranding to ‘Strategy,’ while Eric Trump endorses Bitcoin following mention of Ethereum. Blackrock is set to introduce a Bitcoin exchange-traded product (ETP) in Europe. The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) is planning to revise its guidelines on cryptocurrency banking. The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is to discuss the regulation of prediction markets. In legislative news, New Mexico has introduced a Bitcoin treasury bill, whereas the Czech Republic may take months to implement its Bitcoin treasury. The Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE) is seeking approval for Ethereum options trading. Tether has launched artificial intelligence applications and a software development kit (SDK) platform. MetaMask has implemented a feature for token-based transaction fees. Concerns have been raised about Bitcoin's security budget, as outlined by Drake, while StanChart predicts Bitcoin could reach $500k by 2028. There was a hacking incident on Jupiter’s X aimed at promoting a meme coin. Virtune has launched exchange-traded products (ETPs) for Aave and Cardano. Additionally, Coinbase has added Ethereum Filecoin (ETHFI) to its listings. Lastly, Canada is increasing costs for perpetual trading.

news.headline: Crypto ETFs are incoming, NFP data today, Altcoin sentiment negative. Trump’s Truth.Fi to launch ‘BTC PLUS’ ETF, SOL possibly reaching $520. XRP ETFs approved, SOL may uptick. XRP CEO could join advisory council, Iowa mulls BTC reserve bill. ETH targets Pectra upgrade in April. HYPE sees record inflows, stablecoin TVL $750m. Justin Sun sparks HTX funding feud, Japan asks Google for app removal.
news.summary: Truth.Fi, founded by Trump, set to launch the 'BTC PLUS' ETF, as SEC acknowledges Grayscale's LTC, SOL, and XRP ETFs. A prediction from VanEck suggests SOL could reach $520 by the end of the year. Various entities are applying for spot XRP ETFs. Franklin Templeton is preparing to unveil a Crypto Index ETF as a crypto de-banking hearing commences. Iowa introduces a BTC reserve bill, while Utah may become the first state to implement a BTC reserve. The CEO of XRP is potentially joining Trump's advisory council. Trump Jr. believes crypto is crucial for future US dominance. ETH is eyeing an April rollout for the Pectra upgrade but faces tough competition according to JP Morgan. HYPE experiences a surge in inflows, with stablecoin TVL hitting $750 million. There are hints suggesting Kraken may have information on Satoshi Nakamoto's identity. Justin Sun sparks controversy over HTX funding disparity, and Ondo plans to launch a Layer 1 blockchain for TradFi. The Winklevoss-owned Gemini exchange is contemplating IPO options. Czechia introduces lower taxes on crypto, while USDT is slated to be used in real estate transactions in the UAE. Furthermore, Japan requests Google to remove crypto exchange apps.

news.headline: Rat Pythia wows with ability to communicate with humans through chip connected to AI, answering yes/no questions. Community cheered.
news.summary: A cutting-edge research laboratory made waves in the cryptocurrency world following an experiment involving a rat named Pythia, which captured the attention of a meme coin community. Pythia has been implanted with a chip directly connected to its brain, linking it to an advanced AI system capable of translating human languages into neural signals. This unique setup allows Pythia to respond to queries necessitating binary answers with either a "yes" or "no."

news.headline: SOL drops, co-founder leaves Meteora, Hyperliquid launches! BTC up in dominance, SOL down 16% in a week. Texas holds BTC reserve hearing today. BTC forecasted to reach $200k by 2025 & $1.5m by 2030. ETH ETF inflows surpass BTC in February. JUP to conduct internal LIBRA investigation, buybacks postponed. Various LIBRA-linked people exposed by DeFiTuna. HK company doubles shares with 1 BTC purchase. Tether works on Neuralink-like brain chip. Monad to testnet tomorrow. Europe MiCA aims to align with US policies.
news.summary: Bitcoin dominance has increased, with SOL experiencing a 16% decrease in the past week. A public hearing regarding Bitcoin reserves will be held in Texas today. Market analyst Van Straten predicts an upcoming breakout for BTC. Financial forecasters predict BTC to reach $200k in 2025 (Bernstein) and $1.5m by 2030 (Ark). In February, ETH ETF inflows surpass those of BTC. MEMX exchange seeks to list 21Shares XRP ETF. Sentiment towards SOL remains negative due to issues with LIBRA and unlocking procedures. DeFiTuna uncovers connections between various individuals and LIBRA. Co-founder of Meteora tied to LIBRA resigns. Jupiter to conduct an internal investigation on LIBRA with buybacks on hold for community feedback. Hyperliquid launches EVM mainnet. Pi Network's search interest hits a new peak as a Hong Kong firm's shares double after purchasing 1 BTC. Tether works on a Neuralink-style brain chip. Monad set to launch testnet tomorrow. Europe's MiCA plan aims to bridge policy gaps with the US.

news.headline: BTC hits $93.5k then bounces back, as alts surge. Google explores BTC adoption. Strategy aims to raise $2bn for BTC buys. Tether prints $1bn USDT. Crypto execs appointed in key roles. Secrets exposed on Libra scam. XRP ETF recognized by SEC. Tether founder's new project. Hong Kong eyes gold tokenization. Schwab appoints digital assets chief. Wintermute expands in US. Bullish gains HK license.
news.summary: Bitcoin surged to a high of $93,500 before experiencing a rebound, with other cryptocurrencies following suit. Google is exploring ways to facilitate the adoption of Bitcoin. A strategy is being implemented to raise an additional $2 billion for the purchase of Bitcoin. FTX has already distributed $800 million to 160,000 individuals. Tether has minted an additional $1 billion USDT on the Tron blockchain and is set to acquire a significant share in a major agricultural company. A supporter of cryptocurrencies, Lutnick, has been appointed as the commerce secretary. The scam involving LIBRA is connected to Milei's sister. The SEC has recognized the XRP ETF. A co-founder of Tether is working on the development of a stablecoin that generates yields. Hong Kong is exploring gold tokenization. Schwab has established a position for a head of digital assets. Wintermute is planning to expand its operations in the US. Bullish has obtained a license in Hong Kong.

news.headline: Altcoins surge, SEC dropping Coinbase suit, KAITO & IP rise!BTC nears $100k, ETH excels. Altcoins gaining as meme trend fades. SEC to drop lawsuit against Coinbase. 1m BTC may offset $21T debt: VanEck. US to acquire 4.2m BTC for reserves: Saylor. Arizona BTC reserve bill heading to Senate. Utah BTC bill vote in May. IP up 150%, leads new altcoins. Sonic's DEX volume up 85%, S up 60%. Some nations blocked from FTX payouts. First African firm buys $10m BTC. LTC ETF listed on DTCC. SBF seeks pardon from Trump. SEC backs yield-bearing stablecoins.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) is approaching the $100,000 mark while Ethereum (ETH) continues to show strong performance. As meme activity wanes, altcoins begin to surge. The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is expected to drop their lawsuit against Coinbase. VanEck suggests that a reserve of 1 million BTC could help offset the massive $21 trillion debt. MicroStrategy CEO Michael Saylor announces the acquisition of 4.2 million BTC for US reserves. Arizona is making progress with a BTC reserve bill heading to the Senate, and Utah is set to vote on a similar bill in May. Intellectual Property (IP) has seen a significant increase of 150% this week, driving the growth of new altcoins. Weekly decentralized exchange (DEX) volume on Sonic has risen by 85%, while the value of 'S' has increased by 60%. FTX payouts are being blocked in multiple countries. The first public African company has purchased $10 million worth of BTC. A Litecoin (LTC) exchange-traded fund (ETF) has been listed on the Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC). FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried is seeking a pardon from former President Trump. The SEC has approved yield-bearing stablecoins.

news.headline: Crypto plunges post-hack, $1.4bn Bybit hack attributed to North Korea's Lazarus. Safe sees musking attack, 0xInfini hacked for $50m. SOLBTC at lowest since Dec 2023. Franklin Templeton files for SOL ETF. Berachain, Sonic see uptrend, BERA leads TVL. Ethena raises $100m for tradfi, Babylon TGE nearing. ARB votes 7.5k ETH in non-ARB protocols, Montana against BTC reserve. El Salvador halts BTC purchases. Hong Kong Asia Holdings, Arkham trading more BTC, Strategy plans $2bn BTC purchase. Monad testnet hits 10k TPS.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency prices dropped recently due to a widespread sell-off in the market and a significant hacking incident. It was revealed that North Korea’s Lazarus group was responsible for a $1.4 billion hack on the Bybit platform. This hack was made possible by a phishing attack on the Safe website. Bybit managed to restore its Ethereum reserves through loans and a buyback program and is offering a 10% reward for the return of any stolen funds. A portion of the hacked funds has already been blocked. Following the hack, Safe has announced plans to enhance its security measures. The hacker now holds more Ethereum than the Ethereum Foundation itself.Additionally, another platform called 0xInfini was hacked for $50 million. Despite calls for an Ethereum rollback in response to the hacks, the idea has been widely dismissed. The SOLBTC pair fell to its lowest level since December 2023, prompting investment firm Franklin Templeton to file for a SOL ETF. Binance sold substantial amounts of SOL through Wintermute, while Berachain and Sonic saw significant inflows. BERA surpassed ARB and Base in terms of Total Value Locked (TVL).Ethena raised $100 million to expand its traditional finance services, with the Babylon Token Generation Event (TGE) expected soon. The ARB protocol voted to invest 7.5k ETH in non-ARB projects, while Montana rejected the proposal to establish a Bitcoin reserve. El Salvador is planning to halt its BTC purchases, but a strategy is in place to acquire an additional $2 billion worth of Bitcoin. Hong Kong Asia Holdings also intends to increase its BTC holdings. In other news, Arkham's spot trading is now live in several states, and the Monad testnet has been launched with a capacity of 10k transactions per second.

news.headline: BTC hits 3-month low, ETF outflows prompt plunge. Crypto loss triggers $1.3bn liquidations. ETH nears 'Death Cross,' S. Dakota, Ohio make crypto moves. SEC, regulation updates, OKX fined $500m. HBAR, ADA ETF in works. Citadel to enter crypto trading. Dubai accepts USDC, EURC.
news.summary: Bitcoin has reached a three-month low due to ETF outflows, with most coins experiencing a similar downtrend since before the "Trump pump." US Bitcoin ETFs saw the second highest outflows of the year. This crypto plunge caused $1.3 billion in liquidations and elevated the Greed and Fear index to 'Extreme Fear.' Ethereum is on the brink of a 'Death Cross' pattern. South Dakota rejected a Bitcoin reserve proposal following Montana. Ohio is contemplating a bill to exempt crypto from state taxes. Bybit repaid Bitget's 40,000 ETH loan. The SEC recognized Grayscale's ADA ETF and Canary Capital filed for an HBAR ETF. Michael Saylor proposed regulatory reforms to the SEC's task force. The strategy premium for Bitcoin holdings has decreased to just 1.6 times. Ethereum's Pectra testnet encountered issues with missing blocks. SBF, tweeting from prison, commended Elon Musk's support for DOGE. The SEC concluded its investigation into Robinhood's crypto offerings. Citadel ventures into crypto trading, while CZ disclosed his private portfolio. Metaplanet purchased an additional $12.9 million worth of Bitcoin. OKX faced a fine exceeding $500 million for unlawfully serving the US market. Infini, a crypto Neo-Bank, suffered a $50 million exploit. South Korea imposed sanctions on Upbit. Dubai now recognizes USDC and EURC under its token regulatory framework.

news.headline: CRYPTO NEWS: UNDER EXPOSED EP14 covers Black Tuesday in crypto market turmoil. Tune in live Tuesdays 12-1 PM EST on YouTube/X.
news.summary: Join UNDER EXPOSED for in-depth coverage of the week's major macroeconomic news affecting crypto and other markets. Hosted by TylerD, Deeze, Geebz, and Peter Jennings, the show delves into emerging trends, trades, and narratives. Tune in live on Tuesdays from 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM EST on YouTube and X for detailed discussions on key sectors in crypto and NFTs, market movers, and investment decisions. Don't miss out on the latest insights and analysis from the experts.

news.headline: Updates on ties between India and US, Luxury Industry changes, and challenges with regulations discussed on Wall Street Week's Bloomberg.
news.summary: Bilateral ties between India and the United States have seen significant growth, with trade volumes exceeding $150 billion. The luxury industry is undergoing a transformation, with innovative approaches and strategies being implemented. However, challenges related to regulations are emerging, potentially impacting the sector's growth.

news.headline: Trader who predicted FTX’s collapse sees 2-year repayment delay as a victory due to complex legal challenges.
news.summary: An industry expert described the creditor repayments for FTX taking place just over 2 years post the unexpected collapse of the company as a positive outcome, considering the challenges related to jurisdictional issues.

news.headline: FDIC must face SVB Financial lawsuit claiming $1.9B.
Ingram Micro CFO to speak at Morgan Stanley Conference on March 5th at 9:15 am PT / 12:15 pm ET. Webcast available.
news.summary: Ingram Micro Holding Corporation (NYSE: INGM) released information stating that their Chief Financial Officer, Mike Zilis, will be engaged in a fireside chat at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media, and Telecom Conference on March 5th, at 9:15 a.m. Pacific Time / 12:15 p.m. Eastern Time. Interested parties can access a live webcast and replay of the chat on the company’s investor relations website at https://ir.ingrammicro.com.

news.headline: K-Bro Linen Inc. will release Q4 2024 financial results on March 20, 2025. Conference call/webcast on March 21 at 9 a.m. ET. Key execs to discuss.参888-510-2154 / 437-900-0527 to join call.
news.summary: K-Bro Linen Inc. (TSX:KBL) will announce its financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, on March 20, 2025, after market close. A conference call and webcast has been scheduled for March 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. ET (7:00 a.m. MT), featuring Linda McCurdy, President and CEO, and Kristie Plaquin, CFO. Analysts and institutional investors can ask questions post the prepared remarks. To join the call, participants can dial 1-888-510-2154 (Canada and USA) or 437-900-0527 (International) or register at https://link.meetingpanel.com/?id=74014 for an instant call back. Replay available for absentees.

news.headline: Larry Summers and Scott Bessent discuss US aid increase to Ukraine amid tensions. -Bloomberg #LarrySummers #ScottBessent #Ukraine #Aid #US
State Street, Apollo to rename Private Debt ETF on SEC concern
Former SSA director advises saving money due to concerns of Social Security cuts.
South Korea semiconductor exports decline 3% year on year in February
EverGen Infrastructure Corp. updates operations at PCR and Prairie Sky Organics. Investments in facility improvements made since acquiring in 2021.
news.summary: EverGen Infrastructure Corp, a company listed on TSXV (EVGN) and OTCQX (EVGIF), has provided operational updates for its PCR and Prairie Sky Organics facilities. Following its acquisition in 2021, EverGen has made investments in environmental improvements at the Pacific Coast Renewables plant to meet stringent regulatory requirements in anticipation of expansion. These enhancements demonstrate EverGen's commitment to sustainability and compliance with industry regulations.

news.headline: Bessent urges Canada to align with Mexico's stance on China tariffs, per Bloomberg's report on 02/28/2025.
news.summary: Bessent is urging Canada to mirror Mexico's proposal regarding tariffs on Chinese goods. This call for action was made on February 28, 2025, during Bloomberg's 'The Close' segment. The specifics of Mexico's pitch in terms of tariff rates or percentages were not specified in the report.

news.headline: Pivot Bio launches new product for US Cotton, accessible to growers nationwide. Feb. 28, 2025.
news.summary: Pivot Bio has expanded its latest technology to farmers and collaborators throughout the U.S. Cotton Belt. The company, based in Wayzata, Minnesota, revealed on February 28, 2025, through PRNewswire, that it has officially introduced a new product for cotton cultivation.

news.headline: Bill Abbott discusses future of values-driven entertainment at NRB 2025 convention in Texas. CEO of Great American Media.
news.summary: In Grapevine, Texas on Feb. 28, 2025, Bill Abbott, President & CEO of Great American Media, participated in the NRB 2025 International Christian Media Convention as a panelist in the Vision for the Future of Values-Driven Media forum. Abbott was among industry leaders discussing key topics at the event, which attracted a large audience and featured various sessions on the importance of values-based media in the modern landscape.

South Korea February trade shifts to surplus of $4.300 billion; estimated at +$4.841 billion
S. Korean Exports Prelim Actual 1% (Forecast 3.7%, Previous -10.2%)

Roughly 7,000 workers to be cut by Social Security Administration as they restructure and close six regional offices.
news.summary: The organization is undergoing changes by reorganizing and shutting down six regional offices that offer coordination and assistance to employees who cater to beneficiaries.

news.headline: Zelensky indicates Ukraine is prepared for minerals deal following dispute with Trump in Oval Office.
i-80 Gold completes steps for recapitalization plan: convertible debenture indenture amended, private placement closed.
news.summary: Update on the amendments to the convertible debenture indenture and conclusion of the concurrent private placement have been finalized. This release serves as a "designated news release" related to the Company's prospectus supplement dated August 12, 2024, concerning its short form base shelf offering.

news.headline: Brookfield Renewable files 2024 annual reports with financial statements for year ended Dec. 31. NYSE: BEP, BEPC; TSX: BEP.UN, BEPC.
news.summary: Today, on February 28, 2025, Brookfield Renewable (NYSE: BEP, BEPC; TSX: BEP.UN, BEPC) released its 2024 annual reports, which comprise audited financial statements for the period ending December 31, 2024. The reports were officially filed on Forms 20-F with the SEC on EDGAR and with Canadian securities regulatory authorities on SEDAR+.
